# weekly_activity_report

Weekly summary of call centre operations for the CanadaLogin support queue.

Each row represents one reporting week, summarizing call volume, service levels,
call outcomes, and call type breakdowns for that week.

`Keywords`: call centre, calls, weekly, volume, wait time, call length, service level

## Provenance

Sourced from a weekly Excel report that is typically send to the EDCP Data and Research 
team via email. That spreadsheet is exctracted and loaded into the data repository via
the [call centre weekly activity report](https://github.com/cds-snc/canadalogin-ibm-verify-pipeline/blob/main/infra/modules/upload_source/call_centre_weekly_activity_report/lambda_src/handler.py)
Lambda. The `extracted_at` field records when each row was last extracted 
from the source file.

* **Updated**: Weekly, typically on Tuesdays
* **Steward**: EDCP Data and Research Team
* **Contact**: [Colin Douglas](mailto:colin.douglas@cds-snc.ca)

## Fields

One row per reporting week.

Many count fields are stored as `float` because they originate from Excel.
Proportions (fields ending in `_pct`) are stored as values between 0 and 1,
not 0 and 100.

| Field Name | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| week_text | string | Report title, extracted from the Excel file |
| date_range_start | string | Start date of the reporting week as a text date (for example, "2026-04-26") |
| date_range_end | string | End date of the reporting week as a text date (for example, "2026-05-02") |
| date | string | Equivalent to `date_range_start`; used as the primary date key |
| file | string | Source filename the row was extracted from |
| extracted_at | string | ISO 8601 timestamp of when the row was extracted from the source file |
| calls_accepted | float | Total calls accepted by the queue during the week |
| calls_accepted_pct | float | Proportion of calls accepted (typically 1.0) |
| calls_abandoned | float | Number of calls abandoned before being answered |
| calls_abandoned_pct | float | Proportion of accepted calls that were abandoned |
| calls_answered | float | Number of calls answered by an agent |
| pct_answered_within_60s | float | Proportion of calls answered within 60 seconds |
| avg_delay_seconds | float | Average wait time before a call was answered, in seconds |
| avg_call_length_seconds | float | Average duration of answered calls, in seconds |
| avg_handle_time_seconds | float | Average total handle time per call (call length plus any wrap-up time), in seconds |
| calls_completed_by_io | float | Number of calls completed by the information officer |
| calls_completed_by_io_pct | float | Proportion of answered calls completed by the information officer |
| hangups | float | Number of calls where the caller hung up |
| hangups_pct | float | Proportion of answered calls that ended in a hangup |
| test_calls | float | Number of test calls made during the week |
| test_calls_pct | float | Proportion of accepted calls that were test calls |
| total_calls_answered | float | Total calls answered (may differ from `calls_answered` depending on reporting logic) |
| total_calls_answered_pct | float | Proportion of accepted calls that were answered |
| comments | float | Number of calls where a comment was recorded |
| comments_pct | float | Proportion of answered calls with a comment |
| callback | float | Number of calls where a callback was requested or offered |
| callback_pct | float | Proportion of answered calls that involved a callback |
| no_information_provided | float | Number of calls where no information was provided to the caller |
| no_information_provided_pct | float | Proportion of answered calls where no information was provided |
| transfer_to_crisis_centres | float | Number of calls transferred to a crisis centre |
| transfer_to_crisis_centres_pct | float | Proportion of answered calls transferred to a crisis centre |
| program_content_provided | float | Number of calls where program content was provided to the caller |
| program_content_provided_pct | float | Proportion of answered calls where program content was provided |
| total_of_call_types | float | Total number of call-type records for the week (used as denominator for call-type proportions) |
| total_of_call_types_pct | float | Proportion of total call types (typically 1.0) |

## Notes

`avg_delay_seconds` and `avg_call_length_seconds` are weekly averages sourced
from the report directly. When combining across weeks, use a weighted average
with `calls_answered` as the weight rather than a simple average. See the
[Call Centre metrics](../../02-call-centre.qmd) chapter for example SQL.

`date_range_start` and `date_range_end` are stored as strings. Cast to `DATE`
in SQL when filtering by date range.
